name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-4
title: Skills-updated alert fires even when no skill content changed
description: After merging PR-79 (which has no SKILL.md changes), the Stop hook emitted: '[rabbit] Skills updated: rabbit-feature-touch,rabbit-workspace-map,rabbit-feature-scope,rabbit-file — will reload automatically on next invocation.' No SKILL.md file was modified in the PR — only hooks, scripts, spec, and tests changed. Investigation needed: per spec invariant 24(a), build.sh writes .rabbit-plugins-stale whenever it copies any target under .claude/skills/ (or commands/ or agents/), regardless of whether the content actually changed. If the spec intends the alert to fire only on genuine content changes (new or modified skills), then build.sh must compare source vs destination before writing the marker — not write it unconditionally on every copy run. If the spec allows the alert on any build.sh invocation that touches skills targets (even no-op copies), then the alert behavior is correct but misleading. Needs clarification: is .rabbit-plugins-stale supposed to signal 'build ran' or 'skill content actually changed'?

history.note: Stale: bug symptom no longer reproducible. Root cause was spec/impl naming divergence (Invariant 24 referenced .rabbit-plugins-stale; impl uses .rabbit-skills-updated). Spec updated to match implementation in commit d55ba35. Tests green: 10/10 and 7/7.

Reopened: BUG-4 fix (d55ba35) was spec-only. Root cause confirmed still present: build-targets.py calls shutil.copy2 unconditionally then writes .rabbit-skills-updated for every SKILL.md target, regardless of whether content changed. Alert fires on every build run even when no SKILL.md differs from its source. Fix required: compare sha256(source) vs sha256(destination) before copying; only write marker when content actually changes.

Fixed in 18126e4: build-targets.py now sha256-gates both shutil.copy2 and .rabbit-skills-updated marker write. No-op copies (identical content) produce no marker; alert fires only on genuine SKILL.md content changes.
